diff:
diff --git a/src/MediatR/Wrappers/NotificationHandlerWrapper.cs b/src/MediatR/Wrappers/NotificationHandlerWrapper.cs
index 404fe3f..be59387 100644
--- a/src/MediatR/Wrappers/NotificationHandlerWrapper.cs
+++ b/src/MediatR/Wrappers/NotificationHandlerWrapper.cs
@@ -23,8 +23,7 @@ public class NotificationHandlerWrapperImpl<TNotification> : NotificationHandler
     {
         var handlers = serviceFactory
             .GetServices<INotificationHandler<TNotification>>()
-            .GroupBy(static x => x.GetType())
-            .Select(static g => g.First())
+            .Distinct(ReferenceEqualityComparer.Instance)
             .Select(static x => new NotificationHandlerExecutor(x, (theNotification, theToken) => x.Handle((TNotification)theNotification, theToken)));
 
         return publish(handlers, notification, cancellationToken);

exploration_log.aha_moment: The only true duplicate that should be removed is when DI resolves the exact same object instance twice (e.g., a singleton registered multiple times). By definition, two different instances of the same type with different config are not duplicates.
exploration_log.hindsight: The fix is a direct translation of the task description — no investigation of callers needed.

alternatives: Keep GroupBy but only deduplicate same reference
key_decisions.reasoning: ReferenceEqualityComparer.Instance is built into .NET 5+ and is the idiomatic way to compare by object identity. It's a drop-in replacement that exactly matches the fix described in the task.

search_sequence.reasoning: Task description points directly to this file and the GroupBy dedup logic
search_sequence.result: Found the exact buggy pattern: .GroupBy(x => x.GetType()).Select(g => g.First())

queries.query_text: replace GroupBy dedup with Distinct ReferenceEqualityComparer in NotificationHandlerWrapperImpl to allow multiple same-type handler registrations

solve_notes: Replaced the `GroupBy(GetType()).Select(g => g.First())` deduplication with `Distinct(ReferenceEqualityComparer.Instance)`. The old code collapsed all instances of the same type down to one, discarding intentional multi-registrations with different state. The new code only removes exact same-reference duplicates.
task_text:
Fix NotificationHandlerWrapperImpl dedup logic removing legitimate multiple registrations

In `src/MediatR/Wrappers/NotificationHandlerWrapper.cs`,
`NotificationHandlerWrapperImpl<TNotification>.Handle` groups handlers
by `GetType()` and takes `First()` from each group. This deduplication
removes intentional multiple registrations of the same handler type
with different constructor parameters (e.g., via factory registrations
with different configuration). Fix the dedup to only remove exact
reference duplicates using `Distinct()` with `ReferenceEqualityComparer`
instead of grouping by type.

test_selection.coverage_skip_reason: Auditor could not generate baseline coverage — see auditor report

thrash_preventing_defs.name: NotificationHandlerExecutor
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: verifying the structure of NotificationHandlerExecutor confirms it wraps a handler instance, and that the dedup happens before wrapping — no changes needed there

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is just the one edited method. An AI agent might inspect NotificationHandlerExecutor to understand whether dedup should happen before or after wrapping, and to confirm the executor holds per-instance state (which would confirm the reference-equality approach). Without seeing it upfront, the agent might waste a search verifying this assumption.
